data.title: /dso:implementation-plan does not return control to sprint orchestrator after gap analysis
data.description:
## Description

When /dso:implementation-plan is invoked from /dso:sprint via the Skill tool, it completes gap analysis and outputs STATUS:complete but does not return control flow back to the sprint orchestrator. The sprint orchestrator waits indefinitely for the skill to finish.

## Evidence

From host project log: implementation-plan completed all steps including gap analysis (6 findings processed), output STATUS:complete with 8 task IDs, but the sprint orchestrator did not resume. The skill's output was visible in the conversation but the Skill tool invocation did not terminate.

## Expected Behavior

After outputting `STATUS:complete TASKS:<ids> STORY:<id>`, the /dso:implementation-plan skill should terminate cleanly, returning control to the calling sprint orchestrator so it can parse the STATUS line and continue with batch preparation.

## Possible Root Cause

The skill may be waiting for user input or continuing with additional steps after outputting the STATUS line, rather than stopping after the output protocol section. The Output Protocol section says "output one of these STATUS lines as the final output" but the skill may have additional logic after Step 6 that prevents termination.

## Severity

P2 — affects sprint flow but workaround exists (manually continuing).
